1.21: Prophecy never originated in human will. Instead, people spoke from God, carried along by the Holy Spirit.
1.18: We ourselves heard that voice from heaven when we were with him on the holy mountain.
1.10: So, brothers and sisters, be even more diligent to make your calling and election secure. If you do these things, you will never stumble—
1.12: So I will always remind you of these things, even though you already know them and are established in the truth.
1.11: and you will receive a grand welcome into the eternal kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ.
1.8: If these qualities are increasingly growing in you, they will keep you from being ineffective and unproductive in your knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
1.16: We did not follow cleverly invented myths when we told you about the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ—we were actual eyewitnesses of his majestic glory.
1.9: But those who lack these qualities are so shortsighted they are practically blind—they have forgotten that they were cleansed from their past sins.
1.19: Because of this, we have even greater confidence in the message of the prophets. Pay careful attention to it, like a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day breaks and the morning star rises in your hearts.

1.1: I, Simon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ, am writing to those who have received the same precious faith as ours—made possible through the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ.
1.4: Through these he has given us his precious and magnificent promises, so that through them you can escape the corruption that sinful desire brings and actually share in God's own nature.
1.3: By his divine power, God has given us everything we need for life and godliness—through knowing him who called us by his own glory and moral excellence.
1.5: For this reason, make every effort to add to your faith goodness; and to goodness, knowledge;
1.20: Above all, understand this: no prophecy of scripture ever came from the prophet's own understanding.
1.6: and to knowledge, self-control; and to self-control, perseverance; and to perseverance, godliness;
1.15: I will do everything I can to make sure you can remember these things even after I am gone.
1.13: I think it is right, while I'm still living in this body, to keep refreshing your memory—
1.2: May grace and peace be multiplied to you through the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord.

2.21: It would have been better for them never to have known the way of righteousness than to know it and then turn away from the holy command they were given.
2.12: But these people are like mindless animals—born by instinct to be caught and destroyed. They mock what they don't understand, and like animals they will be destroyed,
2.1: But there were also false prophets among God's people in the past, just as there will be false teachers among you now. They will secretly introduce destructive heresies—even denying the Master who bought them—bringing swift destruction on themselves.

2.7: But he rescued Lot, a righteous man who was deeply distressed by the obscene behavior of the wicked around him.
2.17: These people are like dried-up springs, like fog driven away by a storm. The blackest darkness has been reserved for them.
2.14: With eyes full of lust, they never stop sinning. They lure unstable people into sin—experts in greed. God's curse is on them!
2.8: Day after day, living among those people, Lot's righteous soul was tormented by what he saw and heard.
2.2: Many will follow their shameful behavior, and the way of truth will be slandered because of them.
2.22: The ancient proverbs describe them perfectly: 'A dog returns to its vomit,' and 'A washed pig goes back to rolling in the mud.'
2.5: He did not spare the ancient world—he brought the flood on those ungodly people, but he preserved Noah, a preacher of righteousness, along with seven others.
2.18: They speak empty, boastful words, and by appealing to people's sinful desires, they lure back those who are just beginning to escape from people who live in error.
2.16: But Balaam was rebuked for his sin—a donkey, which cannot even speak, suddenly spoke with a human voice and stopped the prophet's madness!
2.20: If those who have escaped the world's corruption by knowing our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ are again entangled in it and overcome, they are worse off than they were before.
2.3: In their greed they will use you with their fabricated teachings to make money. Their judgment was set long ago, and their destruction is not sleeping.
2.6: He condemned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah, burning them to ashes as a permanent warning to the ungodly of what is coming.

2.19: They promise freedom while they themselves are slaves to corruption—for you are a slave to whatever controls you.

3.1: Dear friends, this is now my second letter to you. In both letters I am trying to stir up your thinking by way of reminder—
3.14: So then, dear friends, since you are looking forward to this, do everything you can to be found spotless, blameless, and at peace with him.
3.15: Remember that the Lord's patience is for the purpose of salvation—just as our dear brother Paul wrote to you with the wisdom God gave him.
3.17: So, dear friends, you've been warned in advance. Be on your guard—don't be carried away by the error of lawless people and fall from your secure position.
3.8: Don't overlook this important fact, dear friends: with the Lord, a single day is like a thousand years, and a thousand years are like a single day.
3.11: Since everything will be dissolved this way, what kind of people should you be? You should live holy and godly lives—
3.2: I want you to recall the words spoken long ago by the holy prophets and the command our Lord and Savior gave through your apostles.
